commodities.name: Wheat
commodities.detail: $6.12/bu (May 8 CBOT July). Bloomberg: food prices at 3-year high. Below farmer break-even (~$7.96/bu). Fertilizer up 20-40% since war began. USDA forecasts food-at-home up 3.1% in 2026.

commodities.name: Corn
commodities.detail: Corn $4.68/bu (May 8 CBOT July). Below farmer break-even (~$5/bu). Driven by fertilizer/transport costs from Hormuz disruption. Bloomberg: food prices at highest in 3+ years.

commodities.name: Soybeans
commodities.detail: Soybeans $11.84/bu (May 8 CBOT, +0.58% on day). Below break-even (~$12.27/bu). Soybean oil at historic highs on biofuel demand. Hormuz disruptions boosting biodiesel.

commodities.name: Rice
commodities.detail: Asian import routes through Persian Gulf completely disrupted. Export restrictions emerging in Southeast Asia.

commodities.name: Sugar
commodities.detail: ICE Sugar #11 at $0.224/lb (April 25). Up 6.7% from pre-war. USDA forecasts sugar & sweets +6.7% in 2026.

commodities.name: Cooking Oil
commodities.detail: Vegetable oil price index at highest since June 2022. Palm oil (CPO June) RM 4,535/tonne (April 11, down 2.33% — five-week rally snapped). Indonesia CPO IDR 16,049/kg.

commodities.name: Coffee
commodities.detail: Arabica $2.83/lb (May 1). Down from 2025 peak of $4.41 — Brazil 2026/27 harvest projected +12% easing pressure. Down 27.5% YoY. Container rates still up 85% on key routes.

commodities.name: Beef
commodities.detail: Feed costs up due to grain price spikes. Diesel-dependent supply chain passing costs to consumers.

groceryImpact.usdaForecast2026: 3.1% food-at-home (USDA update, nearly double Jan projection), rising energy costs may push higher
groceryImpact.note: USDA forecasts food-at-home up 3.1% in 2026 (nearly double Jan projection). Bloomberg: food prices at highest in 3+ years. Beef/veal +12.1% YoY. 80%+ consumers shifting shopping behavior. Tehran wheat flour up 120% in one month. If Hormuz persists through summer, could add 5-7 more percentage points.

fertilizer.detail: Urea up 47% since February (Yara CEO, May 1). Half a million tonnes of nitrogen fertilizer not being produced. Yara warns Iran conflict could put 10 billion meals per week at risk from fertilizer shortages. Ammonia prices up 41% YoY. 25% of US farmers have NOT purchased fertilizer for planting season. USDA projects 3.6% food price rise in 2026.

context: Day 71 5:13 PM PT: Bloomberg: food prices at highest in 3+ years. USDA forecasts food-at-home up 3.1% in 2026. Wheat $6.12/bu (+8.9%). Corn $4.68/bu (+8.6%). Soybeans $11.84/bu (+17%). Brent $101.73 (+27.5%), WTI $95.46 (+26.4%). Gas $4.55 (+49.2%). Consumer sentiment record low 48.2. Hormuz Day 68 blockade.
